description: Phase 0 - Repo Bootstrap: Convert the skeleton monorepo into a real pnpm + Turborepo workspace with ESLint, Prettier, Vitest, Husky, commitlint, CI/CD pipeline, and proper project configs. This establishes the build/test/lint foundation for all subsequent work.
steps: Create a new feature branch: git checkout -b feat/production-ready-phase0-phase1
Create pnpm-workspace.yaml at root listing 'apps/*' and 'packages/*' as workspace members

Rewrite root package.json: add devDependencies (typescript@~5.5.0, turbo@^2, vitest@^2, @vitest/coverage-v8, eslint@^9, prettier@^3, husky@^9, lint-staged@^15, @commitlint/cli@^19, @commitlint/config-conventional@^19), add proper scripts (lint, typecheck, test, build, format, prepare), set packageManager field to pnpm
Create turbo.json with pipeline: lint (no deps), typecheck (depends on ^typecheck), test (depends on ^build), build (depends on ^build), dev (cache false, persistent true)
Create .nvmrc with content '22'
Create .editorconfig with standard settings (utf-8, lf, 2-space indent for ts/tsx/json/yaml/yml, 4-space for md)
Create .gitignore covering node_modules, .turbo, .next, dist, build, coverage, .env, .env.*, !.env.example, *.log, .DS_Store, .prisma
Create .prettierrc.json with shared config (semi: true, singleQuote: true, trailingComma: 'all', printWidth: 100, tabWidth: 2)
Create .prettierignore (node_modules, dist, build, coverage, .turbo, pnpm-lock.yaml, prisma/migrations)

Create eslint.config.mjs using flat config with @typescript-eslint/parser, typescript-eslint plugin, eslint-plugin-import for import ordering. Keep rules reasonable - no-unused-vars as warn, consistent-type-imports as warn. Do NOT lint existing placeholder code strictly - use ignorePatterns for apps/ and most packages/ initially.
Create commitlint.config.js extending @commitlint/config-conventional
Create .husky/pre-commit running lint-staged, and .husky/commit-msg running commitlint
Create .lintstagedrc.json to run prettier --write on staged ts/tsx/json/md files
Create vitest.config.ts at root with workspace configuration pointing to packages/*/vitest.config.ts
Create packages/database/vitest.config.ts as the first test-enabled package config
Create a simple passing test at packages/database/src/__tests__/setup.test.ts that verifies the test infrastructure works (e.g., expect(1+1).toBe(2))

Create .github/workflows/ci.yml: triggers on push to main and PRs, matrix Node 22, steps: checkout, setup pnpm, install, turbo lint, turbo typecheck, turbo test, turbo build. Use pnpm/action-setup and actions/setup-node with caching.
Create .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md with sections: Description, Changes, Testing, Checklist
Create .github/CODEOWNERS assigning * to the default team
Create SECURITY.md with responsible disclosure process
Create .env.example at root with placeholder keys (DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, JWT_SECRET, etc.)
Update root tsconfig.json: add noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true, noImplicitOverride: true, keep exactOptionalPropertyTypes: false for now (too many existing violations)
Run NODE_OPTIONS="" pnpm install to verify lockfile generation works
Run NODE_OPTIONS="" pnpm turbo test to verify Vitest infrastructure passes
acceptance_criteria: pnpm-workspace.yaml exists and lists apps/* and packages/*
NODE_OPTIONS="" pnpm install succeeds and generates pnpm-lock.yaml
NODE_OPTIONS="" pnpm turbo test runs Vitest and the setup test passes
turbo.json exists with lint, typecheck, test, build pipelines defined
.github/workflows/ci.yml exists with a valid workflow definition
.gitignore exists and covers node_modules, .turbo, dist, coverage, .env
ESLint config, Prettier config, commitlint config all exist at root
verification: NODE_OPTIONS="" pnpm turbo test (exit 0, Vitest runs)
findings: 1. Had to convert all workspace package dependencies from bare '*' to 'workspace:*' protocol for pnpm compatibility. 2. The husky prepare script ran automatically during pnpm install. 3. lint-staged with prettier works correctly on pre-commit hook. 4. Many packages under packages/ dir have no package.json (admin, analytics, data-pipeline, etc) - they are just directories with source files. Only 7 packages have package.json. 5. Vitest shows CJS deprecation warning but tests pass fine. 6. esbuild build script was ignored by pnpm (not approved) - not a blocker.
